genre_refs: Howard Pyle 'The Nation Makers'; American Revolutionary War iconography
description: A slogan graphic from the U.S. Department of Labor that overlays text onto a famous fine-art history painting. The artwork is Howard Pyle's 'The Nation Makers,' depicting a column of Revolutionary War Continental soldiers and militia advancing across a field of battle: ragged patriots in white shirts and tricorn hats march forward carrying muskets with fixed bayonets and shouldered rifles, one beating a red field drum, with a tattered American flag (early stars-and-stripes) and a uniformed officer leading at the left, painted in a warm, heroic, painterly style under a smoke-streaked sky. Across the bottom of the image a small DOL/eagle emblem sits above large white serif capital text reading 'PATRIOTISM, NOT GLOBALISM' set against a subtle dark gradient bar. Propaganda technique: appropriates revered Revolutionary-era patriotic imagery to lend historical and moral weight to the administration's economic-nationalist message, framing 'patriotism' and 'globalism' as opposites. The tweet ('2026 is the year of the Patriot. AMERICA FIRST. AMERICANS FIRST.') reinforces the nationalist framing and ties to the 250th-anniversary theme. Target audience: economic nationalists, blue-collar/American-worker base, patriotic supporters.
